mappings.rationale: Directly covers metabolism (B) including catabolism (B.1), cellular respiration (B.2), anabolism/biosynthesis (B.3), and biochemical reactions.
Enzymes catalyze all metabolic reactions including photosynthesis (A), cellular respiration (B.2), and biosynthetic pathways (B.3).
Covers photosynthesis in green plants (A) including the chloroplast (A.4), photosynthetic pigments (A.5), and the carbon reduction cycle (A.7).
Addresses the molecular basis of metabolism including energy carriers, substrates of catabolism (B.1), and products of anabolism (B.3).
Covers cellular respiration in mitochondria (B.2) and the regulation of metabolic processes (B.4) within cells.
Provides the thermodynamic principles underlying bioenergetics (A.6), energy conservation in cellular respiration (B.2), and free energy in metabolism.
Covers the combustion of food materials (B.2) and metabolism of proteins, vitamins, and dietary components central to bioenergetics.
Relevant to the regulation of metabolism (B.4), covering how metabolic processes are temporally organized and regulated.
Addresses the molecular mechanisms underlying biosynthesis of cell components (B.3) and genetic regulation of metabolism (B.4).
Relevant to photosynthesis (A) through the endosymbiotic origin of chloroplasts, and metabolic exchanges in symbiotic relationships.
